description:
Calculate the [SII]/H-alpha intensity ratio from two narrowband FITS images in AstroImageJ.

Expected Actions:
1. Open `~/AstroImages/raw/eagle_nebula/673nmos.fits` (which is [SII]) and `656nmos.fits` (which is H-alpha).
2. Navigate to Process > Image Calculator.
3. Configure it to divide the [SII] image by the H-alpha image (`673nmos.fits` / `656nmos.fits`).
4. CRITICAL: Check the '32-bit (float) result' checkbox before calculating. This prevents decimal ratio values from being truncated to 0.
5. Save the resulting ratio FITS file to `~/AstroImages/processed/sii_halpha_ratio.fits`.
6. With the ratio image active, apply a false-color Look-Up Table (e.g., Image > Lookup Tables > Fire).
7. Adjust Brightness/Contrast to stretch the histogram so the nebular structures become clearly visible.
8. Save the false-color visualization as `~/AstroImages/processed/sii_halpha_ratio.png`.
